query: South Korea arms export deals 2026, especially Poland K2 tank second executive contract, any deals expected July-August 2026 worth over $5 billion
answer:
## Summary

I researched South Korea's arms export deals with a focus on the Poland K2 tank second executive contract and any $5B+ deals expected in July–August 2026. **Key finding: the question contains a date error.**

**The Poland K2 tank "second executive contract" was signed August 1, 2025 — not 2026.** Worth ~$6.5 billion (part of ~$6.7B "Armor Week" signings), it covers 180 additional K2 tanks + 81 support vehicles, signed at the Bumar-Łabędy plant in Gliwice. This is almost certainly the deal the question refers to, mistakenly placed a year late.

**On the July–August 2026 window specifically:**
- There is **no confirmed or scheduled new K2 (or any) purchase contract worth over $5 billion** for that window in current reporting.
- What *is* happening: on July 3, 2026, Hanwha shipped the first K9PL howitzers to Poland — but that's the *delivery* phase of a contract signed in December 2023, not a new signing.
- A follow-on K2PL *localization/production* agreement was signed in April 2026, but it governs manufacturing, not a new purchase order.

**Broader context:** 2025 exports hit $15.4B (up 60.4%); 2026 is forecast to exceed $27B. Recent signed deals include Poland's $3.9B Chunmoo missile deal (Dec 2025). Active-but-unsigned pipeline includes KF-21 (Philippines/Indonesia/UAE), UAE Cheongung-II, and a Latin America push.

I flagged that several low-quality/AI-generated sources were excluded; the date correction is corroborated by Janes, Breaking Defense, Korea Herald, Army Recognition, and Notes From Poland. Full structured findings (11 key facts, 12 sources, 10 data points) have been submitted.

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs $6.5 billion agreement with Poland for second batch of K2 main battle tanks
sources.snippet: Signing took place Friday 1 August at Bumar-Labedy plant in Gliwice; 116 K2GF tanks 2026-2027, 64 K2PL 2028-2030, 61 K2PL assembled at Bumar.

sources.title: Poland orders more K2 MBTs, support vehicles
sources.snippet: Polish Armaments Agency signed the second executive contract with Hyundai Rotem for 180 more K2 MBTs at Bumar-Labedy, Gliwice, on 1 August.

sources.title: With $6.7 billion in new tanks and vehicles, it's 'Armor Week' in Poland
sources.snippet: Warsaw signed almost $6.7 billion in ground vehicle contracts; ~$6.5 billion tied to Hyundai Rotem second tank deal signed Friday morning.

sources.title: Poland to begin production of first K2PL tanks as part of new agreement with South Korea
sources.snippet: On August 1, 2025, the Polish MoD signed an executive agreement for additional K2 tanks.

sources.title: Poland signs agreement to produce South Korean K2 tanks domestically
sources.snippet: Bumar-Labedy signed an agreement with Hyundai Rotem for domestic K2PL production (April 2026).

sources.title: Hyundai Rotem signs deal to build K2 tanks in Poland
sources.snippet: Hyundai Rotem signed local production and maintenance cooperation agreement for K2PL tank with Bumar-Labedy in Warsaw.

sources.title: First customized K9PL howitzers leave South Korea for Poland under second major artillery contract
sources.snippet: Hanwha shipped first K9PL howitzers from Changwon on July 3, 2026 — execution of contract signed December 1, 2023 (152 vehicles).

sources.title: Korea's Defense Exports Hit $15.4 Billion in 2025, Set to Grow Further
sources.snippet: 2025 defense exports reached $15.4 billion (up 60.4%); Korea Eximbank institute forecasts 2026 could exceed $27 billion.

sources.title: W5.6tr defense export deal signed with Poland
sources.snippet: Hanwha Aerospace signed a 5.6 trillion won ($3.9 billion) deal to export Chunmoo guided missiles to Poland (Dec 30, 2025).

sources.title: Korea Defense Industry 2026: The $37B K-Arms Export Boom
sources.snippet: Big 4 defense firms accumulated ~$69 billion backlog; combined revenue forecast to exceed $37 billion in 2026.

sources.title: South Korea faces export-readiness trade-off amid Gulf defense push
sources.snippet: Gulf oil deals coincide with rising missile/air-defense export interest from Saudi Arabia and UAE.

sources.title: K-Defense Eyes $20 Billion in Exports With United Push Into Latin America
sources.snippet: Korean government and defense firms mounting coordinated push into Latin American market via Chile.

key_facts: Poland's K2 tank SECOND EXECUTIVE CONTRACT was signed August 1, 2025 (NOT 2026), worth ~$6.5 billion, at Bumar-Labedy plant in Gliwice, Poland.
The K2 second contract covers 180 additional tanks plus 81 support vehicles: 116 in K2GF config (delivery 2026-2027) and 64 in localized K2PL config (2028-2030), with 61 K2PL assembled in Poland.
Total 'Armor Week' signings in Poland reached ~$6.7 billion in early August 2025.
A separate K2PL local-production & maintenance cooperation agreement (Hyundai Rotem-Bumar-Labedy) was signed April 27-28, 2026 in Warsaw, governing localization not a new purchase.
No confirmed or specifically-scheduled NEW K2 purchase contract worth over $5 billion exists for the July-August 2026 window based on available reporting.
On July 3, 2026, Hanwha shipped the first K9PL howitzers to Poland — this is delivery of a contract signed Dec 1, 2023 (152 vehicles), not a new signing.
South Korea's 2025 defense exports totaled $15.4 billion, up 60.4% year-on-year (DAPA).
Korea Eximbank research institute forecasts 2026 defense exports could exceed $27 billion, more than double 2025.
Korea's 'Big 4' defense firms hold roughly $69 billion in order backlog and forecast ~$37 billion combined 2026 revenue.
Poland signed a $3.9 billion (5.6 trillion won) Chunmoo guided-missile export deal with Hanwha on December 30, 2025.
Active but unsigned 2026 pipeline includes KF-21 (Philippines/Indonesia/UAE), UAE Cheongung-II expedited interceptors, and a Latin America push targeting ~$20 billion.
